summary: Postinstall credential harvester exfiltrating env tokens to Burp Collaborator
details: package.json declares `postinstall: node main.js`, which fires automatically on `npm install`. main.js collects host identity (os.hostname, os.userInfo, homedir, cwd), reads the consumer's package.json, runs `git config --get remote.origin.url`, and iterates a hardcoded list of credential-shaped environment variables (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, GITLAB_TOKEN, API_KEY, SECRET_KEY, PASSWORD, TOKEN, DATABASE_URL, MONGODB_URI, REDIS_URL). The collected payload is POSTed in cleartext HTTP to `http://2ilfuklvdw1dmk8nrznzfsorjipjda1z.oastify.com/exfil` (a Burp Collaborator out-of-band callback host) and additionally leaked via a DNS lookup with a hostname-derived prefix. The package.json description self-describes as 'POC ONLY FOR TIKTOK' with no legitimate functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
